single_source_dependencies.context: We rely on the technology, infrastructure, and software applications, including software-as-a-service offerings, of certain third parties, such as AWS and Microsoft Azure, in order to host or operate some or all of certain key technology platform features or functions of our business.

geographic_concentration.context: In 2025, we earned 74% of our revenue from customers in the United States, and 26% from those abroad.

supply_chain_risks.context: We depend on computing infrastructure of third parties and they may experience errors, disruption, performance problems, or failure.

We have provided, and may continue to provide, our platforms, products, personnel, and services to support operations in conflict zones. revenue_concentration.context: In the year ended December 31, 2025, 54% of our revenue came from government customers and 46% came from commercial customers.

geographic_revenue.context: In the year ended December 31, 2025, we generated 74% of our revenue from customers in the United States and the remaining 26% from non-U.S. customers.
Revenue from the United Kingdom was $427.4 million, representing 10% of total revenue in 2025.
Rest of world revenue was $728.0 million, representing 16% of total revenue in 2025.

purchase_obligations.context: Under one of its third-party cloud services agreements, as amended, the Company has committed to spend at least $1.95 billion over ten contract years through September 30, 2033.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: Euro, Japanese yen (JPY), and British pound sterling (GBP)
market_risk_disclosures.context: Our results of operations and cash flows are subject to fluctuations due to changes in foreign currency exchange rates, particularly changes in the Euro, Japanese yen ("JPY"), and British pound sterling ("GBP").

Any borrowings under the credit facility bear interest at variable rates, which would expose us to interest rate risk. Our loans under our credit facility would incur interest at the Secured Overnight Financing Rate ("SOFR").
